title: What is Microsoft Fabric?
linkedin_content:
🧵 Microsoft Fabric - Day 1/100

🎯 Confused about what is microsoft fabric?? Let's clear this up!

Microsoft Fabric is Microsoft's unified analytics platform that combines:
• Data integration (pipelines, dataflows)
• Real-time analytics (KQL databases) 
• Data science & ML (notebooks, models)
• Business intelligence (Power BI)
• Data warehousing & lakehouses

All built on a unified OneLake foundation with seamless integration.

💡 Pro Tip: Think of what is microsoft fabric? as the building block for your unified analytics journey.

title: Fabric vs Synapse vs Power BI Premium
linkedin_content:

**Azure Synapse Analytics:**
• Focus: Big data & data warehousing
• Separate compute & storage pools
• SQL-first approach

**Power BI Premium:**

title: Understanding Fabric Capacities and Pricing
linkedin_content:
🧵 Microsoft Fabric - Day 3/100

💡 Want to understand understanding fabric capacities and pricing in simple terms?

Fabric Capacity Units (CUs) determine your compute power:

**F2 (2 CUs)**: Development/testing
**F4 (4 CUs)**: Small workloads
**F8-F16**: Production workloads
**F32-F128**: Enterprise scale
**F256-F2048**: Large enterprise

Key considerations:
• Auto-pause reduces costs
• Burst capacity for peak loads
• Reserved capacity discounts available

💡 Pro Tip: Understanding understanding fabric capacities and pricing will save you hours of troubleshooting later.
